ddls:
CREATE TABLE "domains" (
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each domain */,
  "domain_name" TEXT /* Name of the domain being monitored */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category associated with the domain */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address associated with the domain */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the domain was registered */,
  "last_scan_date" TEXT /* Date of the last security scan */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the domain (e.g., active, suspended, expired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("domain_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_domains_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerabilities" (
  "vulnerability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vulnerability */,
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the domain where the vulnerability was found */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Security score associated with the vulnerability */,
  "first_seen" TEXT /* Date when the vulnerability was first detected */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Date when the vulnerability was last observed */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Detailed severity level of the vulnerability (e.g., critical, high, medium, low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the vulnerability */,
  "remediation_steps" TEXT /* Steps to remediate the vulnerability */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source or tool that detected the vulnerability */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the vulnerability (e.g., open, fixed, ignored) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vulnerability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerabilities_domain_id FOREIGN KEY ("domain_id") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the vulnerability category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the vulnerability category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port entry */,
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the domain associated with the port */,
  "port_number" INTEGER /* Port number used by the domain */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the port (e.g., TCP, UDP) */,
  "service" TEXT /* Service running on the port (e.g., HTTP, SSH) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the port (e.g., open, closed, filtered) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ports_domain_id FOREIGN KEY ("domain_id") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., security analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON field for user permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the domain being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_domain_id FOREIGN KEY ("domain_id") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the domain related to the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., vulnerability, score change) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the notification has been read */,
  "is_important" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the notification is important */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_domain_id FOREIGN KEY ("domain_id") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tracking" (
  "tracking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tracking entry */,
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the domain being tracked */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Security score of the domain at the given timestamp */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the score was recorded */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of score change (e.g., increase, decrease) */,
  "previous_score" INTEGER /* Previous security score before the change */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the score change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tracking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tracking_domain_id FOREIGN KEY ("domain_id") REFERENCES domains ("domain_id")
)
